title: collomia debilis subsp. ipomoea
intro: Collomia debilis subsp. ipomoea, also known as the weak-leaved collomia, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Canada. It is used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as possessing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Collomia debilis subsp. ipomoea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and flowers may be dried and used in various forms of herbal remedies.
